results.title: CanSat 2022 Environmental Test Report | PDF | Vacuum | Thermocouple
results.description: Team Garuda conducted environmental tests on their CanSat project including drop tests, thermal tests, vibration tests, and vacuum tests. The drop test subjected the CanSat to a simulated 30G shock to test the parachute and component mounts.
results.content:
Team Garuda conducted environmental tests on their CanSat project including drop tests, thermal tests, vibration tests, and vacuum tests. The drop test subjected the CanSat to a simulated 30G shock to test the parachute and component mounts.
Team Garuda conducted environmental tests on their CanSat project including drop tests, thermal tests, vibration tests, and vacuum tests. The drop test subjected the CanSat to a simulated 30G shock to test the parachute and component mounts. The thermal test heated the CanSat to 60°C for 2 hours to ensure it could operate in hot conditions.

Last Updated: Oct. 19, 2021 · This conference paper documents environmental testing performed on a 120kg university smallsat. It covers ... EMC testing, vibration testing, and thermal/vacuum testing.
This topic covers thermal vacuum (TVAC) testing of a spacecraft and/or its subsystems. TVAC testing is the most realistic thermal simulation of the flight environment. For this reason, this testing is critical to ensuring mission success and can not be replaced by ambient pressure thermal testing.
During a TVAC test, hardware is subjected to a number of hot-cold cycles in a vacuum environment and system performance is verified through functional tests which are carried out at temperature plateaus. Resources under this topic include standard testing thresholds/requirements, guidance for developing and operating a TVAC chamber, and example test reports. ... Nov. 1, 2021 Rate Edit · Only conducting thermal testing at ambient pressure often yields significantly unrealistic results.
This chapter titled "Thermal Testing" is a comprehensive reference regarding thermal testing of space ... flight hardware. The tests covered are thermal cycling (ambient pressure), thermal vacuum, thermal balance, and burn-in. It includes a description of the elements and stages of the traditional approach, environments, margins, requirements, and required equipment/facilitates.

results.title: Temperature Sensors - Vibration Testing & Control - VRU
results.description: Vibration test engineers may monitor temperature during testing to ensure the ambient/surface temperature does not exceed system limits, especially if the device under test (DUT) or equipment is sensitive to temperature.
results.content:
Vibration test engineers may monitor temperature during testing to ensure the ambient/surface temperature does not exceed system limits, especially if the device under test (DUT) or equipment is sensitive to temperature.
They come in various configurations, including thin-film and wire-wound types, and can be adapted to different test setups, including harsh industrial environments. For vibration testing, RTDs are robust and can withstand moderate mechanical stress and thermal cycling.
Proper environmental control and calibration can mitigate these effects, ensuring reliable measurements. Temperature’s effect on vibration involves interactions between material properties, thermal expansion, lubrication characteristics, and structural dynamics.
Regarding vibration test environments, thermocouples are generally rugged and can withstand harsh environmental conditions, including high vibration, mechanical stress, and thermal cycling.
Vibration test engineers may monitor temperature during testing to ensure the ambient/surface temperature does not exceed system limits, especially if the device under test (DUT) or equipment is sensitive to temperature. The temperature change may be due to equipment exertion/friction, or the engineer may intentionally vary the temperature of their test environment to reflect the end-use environment (EUE).

results.title: Environmental stress screening - Wikipedia
results.description: Method: Vibration (typically 0.04 G2/Hz for 5 minutes/axis) and temperature for 3+ cycles, last one failure-free.
results.content:
Method: Vibration (typically 0.04 G2/Hz for 5 minutes/axis) and temperature for 3+ cycles, last one failure-free.
Environmental stress screening (ESS) refers to the process of exposing a newly manufactured or repaired product or component (typically electronic) to stresses such as thermal cycling and vibration in order to force latent defects to manifest themselves by permanent or catastrophic failure during the screening process.
Testing at the physical environmental conditions (shock, vibration, temperature, altitude, humidity, etc.) that simulate those encountered over the operational life of the component.
Random vibration and temperature cycling have proven to be the most successful forms of ESS in terms of effective flaw precipitation.
Examples: PC board with solid state devices, electric motor, cathode ray tube, pressure vessel.
